toronto.planningContext: Toronto projects often involve tight urban lots, zoning checks, Committee of Adjustment risk, tree protection, neighbour conditions, and careful site access planning.
toronto.projectFit: Best-fit projects include custom home rebuilds, additions, garden suites, multiplex conversions, legal suites, and major renovations in established residential areas.
toronto.approvalFocus: Early review should confirm zoning permissions, gross floor area, setbacks, height, parking, servicing, tree impacts, and building permit drawing requirements.

north-york.planningContext: North York projects commonly balance larger lots, older homes, infill rebuilds, additions, and investor interest in multi-unit or secondary-suite layouts.
north-york.projectFit: Best-fit projects include Willowdale rebuilds, Bayview Village additions, York Mills custom homes, multiplex planning, and permit-ready renovation work.
north-york.approvalFocus: Early review should check lot coverage, height, side-yard setbacks, parking, stormwater, tree protection, and structural scope before pricing construction.

markham.planningContext: Markham projects often require design sensitivity around mature communities, subdivision controls, newer estate lots, heritage-adjacent areas, and municipal review timing.
markham.projectFit: Best-fit projects include Unionville and Angus Glen custom homes, luxury renovations, additions, basement suites, and managed construction for investment properties.
markham.approvalFocus: Early review should confirm zoning, conservation or heritage constraints where relevant, lot grading, servicing, permit drawings, and inspection sequencing.

richmond-hill.planningContext: Richmond Hill owners often plan custom homes, additions, and major renovations on suburban lots where grading, servicing, structure, and finish expectations shape the budget.
richmond-hill.projectFit: Best-fit projects include custom homes, rebuilds, major interior renovations, second-storey additions, walkouts, and construction management for higher-value homes.
richmond-hill.approvalFocus: Early review should check zoning, lot grading, stormwater, setbacks, structural changes, HVAC coordination, and permit drawing completeness.

vaughan.planningContext: Vaughan projects range from estate-style custom homes to additions, basement suites, and light commercial work, often with access, grading, and inspection planning needs.
vaughan.projectFit: Best-fit projects include Kleinburg and Woodbridge custom homes, luxury renovations, additions, secondary suites, and ICI improvement projects.
vaughan.approvalFocus: Early review should confirm municipal zoning, grading, servicing, structural scope, material lead times, and inspection coordination.

mississauga.planningContext: Mississauga projects often combine mature-lot renovations, lake-adjacent custom homes, additions, infill rebuilds, and investment-focused secondary living space.
mississauga.projectFit: Best-fit projects include Lorne Park and Mineola custom homes, Port Credit renovations, additions, garden suites where feasible, and major interior upgrades.
mississauga.approvalFocus: Early review should address zoning, lot grading, conservation or tree constraints where applicable, servicing, structural scope, and permit documentation.

scarborough.planningContext: Scarborough projects frequently involve bungalows, older homes, additions, basement suites, multiplex planning, and value-focused rebuilds across varied lot conditions.
